title: Popular Heights Wine Bar Signing Lease for Permanent Space on Kavanaugh

seo.title: Heights Wine Bar Signing Permanent Lease on Kavanaugh | Metro Little Rock News
seo.description: The Heights wine bar that built a following through pop-ups and private events is signing a lease for a permanent brick-and-mortar location on Kavanaugh Boulevard.

summary: A neighborhood wine bar concept that gained traction through pop-up events and private dinners in the Heights is signing a lease for a permanent location on the Kavanaugh corridor, expected to open this summer with a focused natural wine list and small plates.

body_sections.body: The concept — which has been operating under the name Forty-Eight Wine since late 2025 through pop-up events at local venues — is taking over a 1,400-square-foot space at 5813 Kavanaugh Boulevard. The space was previously a boutique retail shop and will be converted into a bar format with counter seating, a short communal table, and an interior that the owners describe as 'wine bar without the pretension.'

body_sections.heading: What to Expect
body_sections.body: The menu will center on natural, low-intervention, and small-producer wines with roughly 30 bottles on the list at any given time, rotating seasonally. Food will be minimal on purpose — a cheese and charcuterie program, a few rotating small plates. The hours model is evening-focused: Wednesday through Sunday, 4 PM to close. No reservations for bar seating; a small table section will take same-day bookings.

body_sections.heading: Neighborhood Context
body_sections.body: Kavanaugh has added several new food and drink options in recent years — the corridor now anchors the Heights' dining identity alongside the cross-streets near the old Pulaski Heights streetcar route. A dedicated wine bar has been absent from the immediate neighborhood. The closest comparable experience has been the bar programs at existing restaurants, which tend toward cocktails first. Forty-Eight would be the first place in the 72205 zip code where wine is the primary offering.

impact_note: For the Heights, this fills a gap — residents who want a bottle-shop-meets-bar experience have had to drive to other parts of the city. A neighborhood wine bar within walking distance of the denser residential blocks between Kavanaugh and Markham changes that.
